documents.title: Barter Value Basics
documents.content: In unstable economies, durable utility tends to hold value: water treatment supplies, medicines, hygiene goods, repair parts, seeds, fuel, and common batteries. Protect core life-support inventory first and never trade away essentials you cannot replace.

documents.title: Safer Trade Procedures
documents.content: Use neutral meeting points, daylight exchanges, and simple written transaction records when possible. Avoid revealing full inventory or home location. Conduct trades with at least one trusted partner and keep exits visible.

documents.title: Community Stability Principles
documents.content: Long-term resilience depends on trust, fair process, and mutual aid. Establish transparent rules for conflict resolution, shared labor, and scarcity allocation. Stable communities usually outperform isolated households in security and recovery.

documents.title: Operational Security Basics
documents.content: Keep sensitive information on a need-to-know basis. Avoid predictable routines for high-value movement. Minimize visible signs of exceptional stockpiles during periods of instability to reduce targeting risk.

documents.title: Local Ledger and Credit Systems
documents.content: If cash systems degrade, communities can maintain exchange with simple ledgers, vouchers, or time-banking. Keep records auditable and publicly verifiable to preserve trust. Standardize units of value and review balances routinely.
